original: Elon Muskunveils a pig named Gertrude with a coin-sized computer in her brain as part of his Neuralink start-up creating a brain-to-machine interface
Macy's Tap-o-Mania sets a Guinness World Record for tap dancing, with 4,497 participants dancing to "There's No Business Like Show Business"
Michael Faradaydemonstrates the first electric transformer
GuitaristGeorge Harrison(15) joins The Quarrymen, a skiffle band that later becomes The Beatles
Hajime Isayama, Japanese manga artist (Attack on Titan), born in  Ōyama, Japan
Temple Grandin, American animal welfare and autism expert, born in Boston, Massachusetts
Chris Hadfield, Canadian astronaut (STS 74, sk: 99), born in Sarnia, Ontario
Donn Fendler, American author and public speaker, who survived a week in the wilderness aged 12, born in Rye, New York (d. 2016)[1]
Ishi, considered the last Native American to make contact with European Americans, emerges from the wilderness of northeastern California
The synthetic chemical element Meitnerium, atomic number 109, is first synthesized at the Gesellschaft für Schwerionenforschung in Darmstadt, Germany
Star in Cygnus goes nova, becoming the fourth brightest in the sky
British astronomer William Huggins discovers chemical composition of nebulae
Anne McCaffreymakes history as the first woman to win a Hugo Award for Best Novella with her work "Weyr Search" (Dragonflight)

Henry Bergh, American social reformer who founded the American Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (ASPCA) and co-founded the New York Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children (NYSPCC), born in New York City (d. 1888)
Glenn Curtiss of the USA wins the world's first air race, held in Rheims, France, on the final day of the Grande Semaine d'Aviation
German airshipGraf Zeppelinends a round-the-world flight
Seismic sea waves created by the Krakatoa eruption cause a rise in the English Channel 32 hours after the explosion
First white-Indian lacrosse game in Montreal; Indigenous team wins
Dhyan Chand, Indian field hockey player (Olympic gold 1928, 1932, 1936; birthday 29/8 celebrated as India's National Sports Day), born in Allahabad, India (d. 1979)
MLB St. Louis Cardinals'Lou BrockeclipsesTy Cobb's 49-year-old career stolen bases record at 893 as the Padres win 4-3

Australian swimmerDawn Fraserwins the women's 100 m freestyle gold medal at the Rome Olympics, retaining the title won in Melbourne 1956, and will win an unprecedented third consecutive 100 m gold in Tokyo 1964
Bob Beamon, American track and field long jumper (Olympic gold 1968, 29' 2½" 8.9m), born in South Jamaica, Queens, New York
Portuguese athleteRosa Motawins the Women's Marathon at the World Championships in Rome in 2 hours, 25 minutes, and 17 seconds
Phillies minor leaguer Jeff Stone steals a professional baseball record 121st base en route to 122 (Spartanburg, South Atlantic League)
Australia beats England by 7 runs in a match dubbed the "Death of English cricket" as Australian fast bowlerFred Spofforthtakes 14-90 (7-46 and 7-44)
An American sweep of the medals in the men's 100 m freestyle at the Antwerp Olympics; Duke Kahanamoku sets a world record of 1:00.4 in defending his 1912 gold medal

Ethelda Bleibtreywins her third gold medal at the Antwerp Olympics, teaming with Irene Guest, Frances Schroth, and Margaret Woodbridge in the American 4 x 100 m freestyle relay team, setting a world record of 5:11.6
Sandy Neilson wins the women's 100 m freestyle gold medal in 58.59 seconds at the Munich Olympics, beating fellow American Shirley Babashoff andShane Gouldof Australia
Gemini V returns to Earth after 120 orbits and a record 7 days, 22 hours, 55 minutes, and 14 seconds
Atlantis moves to the launch pad for the 51-J mission
USSR launches Soyuz TM-6, including Valery Polyakov, Vladimir Lyakhov, and the first Afghan cosmonaut Abdul Ahad Mohmand, to the Mir station
Hurricane Harvey sets a rainfall record of 51.88 inches in Cedar Bayou from a tropical cyclone in the continental US, according to the US National Weather Service
Hurricane Ida makes landfall as a Category 4 storm near Port Fourchon, Louisiana, on the anniversary of Hurricane Katrina
Parasite worm, alive and wriggling, found in a woman's brain for the first time in Canberra, Australia
The first Carnegie library, financed by industrialistAndrew Carnegie, opens in Dunfermline, Scotland, as the first of 2,509 libraries built around the world
Gottlieb Daimlerreceives a German patent for a motorcycle
Charles F. Kettering, American inventor (auto self-starter), born in Loudenville, Ohio (d. 1958)
Self-governing windmill patented by American inventor Daniel Halladay
Skype Technologies releases its pioneering video-conferencing software
Slaves in the French colony of Saint-Domingue (Haiti) are freed
The Beatles' last public concert takes place before a crowd of 25,000, with 7,000 unsold seats, at Candlestick Park in San Francisco, California
Brazil wins the men's volleyball gold medal with a 3-1 victory over Italy at the Athens Olympics
Houston Astros MLB playerNolan Ryansurpasses the 200-strikeout barrier for a record 11th time
Future undisputed world light heavyweight boxing champion Andre Ward of the US wins the light heavyweight gold medal at the Athens Olympics, defeating Magomed Aripgadjiev of Belarus on points
AviatorAnne Morrow Lindberghmakes her first solo flight
Cliff Richardand the Drifters release their debut single "Move It," credited as the first British rock 'n' roll song
Karen Hesse, American children's book writer (Out of the Dust), born in Baltimore, Maryland
Kumi Tanioka, Japanese video game composer (Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles), born in Hiroshima, Japan
Hiroki Kikuta, Japanese video game composer (Koudelka, Indivisible), born in Aichi Prefecture, Japan
Niccole Byer, American comedian, actress and TV host (Nailed It), born in Middletown Township, New Jersey
Elizabeth Fraser, Scottish rock singer (Cocteau Twins - "Heaven or Las Vegas"), born in Grangemouth, Scotland
Lauren Collins, Canadian actress (Paige Michalchuk in "Degrassi: The Next Generation"), born in Thornhill, Ontario
Dante Basco, Filipino-American actor (Hook, American Dragon: Jake Long), born in Pittsburg, California
Me'Shell Ndegéocello [Michelle Johnson], American singer-songwriter ("If That's Your Boyfriend..."), born in Berlin, Germany
Carla Gugino, American actress (Spy Kids), born in Sarasota, Florida
(Sadenia) "Eddi" Reader, Scottish singer-songwriter (Fairground Attraction - "Find My Love"), born in Glasgow, Scotland
Anton Newcombe, American musician (The Brian Jonestown Massacre), born in Newport Beach, California
Nicholas Tse, Hong Kong singer and actor (New Police Story, Bodyguards and Assassins), born in British Hong Kong
Charlotte Richie, English actress (Call the Midwives; Ghosts; You) and singer, born in Clapham, London
Bizarrap [Gonzalo Julián Conde], Argentine rapper (Quevedo), born in Ramos Mejía, Argentina
